title: Requested, applied, and observed state shall remain distinct modeled concepts
expected_proof: state transition trace plus conformance assertion report

title: Restored profile and workspace state shall not be treated as live machine state
expected_proof: restore scenario trace, event log, no-write assertion

title: Graph live, review, history, and live-trace modes shall remain explicitly distinguishable
Authorization shall remain distinct from UI enablement or visibility
expected_proof: authorization decision trace and UI-state contrast evidence

title: Signal identity shall remain stable across display-name edits and restore cycles
expected_proof: signal registry export and rename-safe restore trace

title: Command request must pass authorization and arbitration before apply is published
Restore operations shall not directly publish physical command apply events
Alarm lifecycle ordering shall preserve assert, acknowledge, and return-to-normal truth
expected_proof: alarm lifecycle trace and historian event set

title: Graph mode transitions shall remain explicit and reversible
expected_proof: mode transition trace and return-to-live trace

title: Derived-signal activation shall be dependency-tracked and cycle-checked before publish
Stale, degraded, and disconnected timing semantics shall remain distinct
Observed confirmation and mismatch timing shall be modeled explicitly
expected_proof: timeout/mismatch timing declaration and event trace

title: Critical commands shall emit attributable event evidence
Alarm lifecycle transitions shall emit complete historian/event evidence
expected_proof: historian export and lifecycle event sequence

title: Restore-origin operations shall be labeled as restore-originated
Graph mode changes and overlays shall leave an evidence trail when exported or reviewed
Derived-signal validation results shall be preserved as reviewable evidence
expected_proof: validation report and dependency graph export

title: Package governance artifacts shall remain synchronized and auditable
